one_liner: APP -6.65% to $527 retreating from the July 1 Raymond James surge — VCP structure intact above $490 floor, DCF $1,184 (55.5% MoS) unchanged; this is the pullback to watch for a re-entry.

news_catalyst.headline: Pullback from July 1 +10.56% Raymond James surge; Broadcom guidance miss creates sector headwinds
news_catalyst.summary: AppLovin's pullback is macro, not stock-specific. No negative news on APP itself — the Broadcom Q3 AI guidance miss and weak jobs print weighed on all elevated-multiple AI/tech names. After a 10.56% surge on July 1 driven by Raymond James's Strong Buy initiation at $640, a 6.65% two-day pullback is normal consolidation. The $524.82 intraday low is near the VCP base that launched the July 1 move; the test is whether prior resistance becomes support.

technical.summary: APP's two-contraction VCP (29.5%→12.5%, both with volume drying) was the setup that triggered the July 1 breakout attempt. Today's pullback to $524.82 is now testing whether the breakout level ($510-520 zone) holds as support. The 1.13x volume on the decline is modest — not institutional distribution. The $490 floor (prior VCP base) remains the ultimate support. Close above $540 tomorrow would confirm healthy consolidation within the VCP structure. DCF at $1,184 vs $527 = 55.5% MoS remains the strongest fundamental anchor for growth tech in the watchlist.

wyckoff_narrative: In Wyckoff terms, after an SOS on July 1 a low-volume pullback to the breakout zone is the ideal LPS (Last Point of Support) pattern before the next advance. Today's 1.13x decline to $524 fits this description. The $490 floor is the Wyckoff stop; a close below it would invalidate the SOS and suggest the July 1 move was a false breakout.

valuation.summary: DCF base $1,184 vs $527 = 55.5% MoS. Raymond James $640 target is well below DCF base case. E-commerce advertising platform represents a significantly larger TAM than mobile gaming alone.

risks: Close below $490 invalidates VCP and signals failed breakout
Broadcom guidance miss creates sector pressure on high-multiple AI names
August 5 earnings: e-commerce platform must show real adoption traction or thesis challenged
